docs.title: Aergia Mod Devkit
docs.text:
This is a devkit library that eliminates boilerplate code. Use annotations to automatically generate component, system registration, and CodecBuilder etc...

  • Set up in 5 minutes

    Add library into your project dependencies, and add ksp configuration!

    Getting started

  • It's made for Kotlin

    Library is made for Kotlin Plugins/Mods and should work with any Server version.

  • Java plugin to Kotlin Guide

    Guide helping you to migrate your Java plugin to Kotlin.

    Java to Kotlin Guide

  • Open Source, MIT

    This project is fully OpenSource, Feel free to contribute or fork and make your own version. Contributing

docs.title: Codec Generation
docs.text:
Writing Codec is boring and looks ugly. Takes a lot of space in the code and it's not very readable. Look at this example, where I wanted to create a component that holds information for Homing Projectile behavior.

To properly serialize/deserialize this component, I need to write a Codec for it, and it looks like this:

docs.title: Before Using the library
docs.text:
Before Using the library

HomingProjectileComponent.java
public class HomingProjectileComponent implements Component<EntityStore> {
    public static final BuilderCodec<HomingProjectileComponent> CODEC = BuilderCodec
            .builder(HomingProjectileComponent.class, HomingProjectileComponent::new)
            .append(new KeyedCodec<>("IsEnabled", Codec.BOOLEAN, true),
                    (homingProjectileConfig, value) -> homingProjectileConfig.isEnabled = value,
                    homingProjectileConfig -> homingProjectileConfig.isEnabled
            )
            .documentation("Enable/Disable Homing Feature")
            .add()
            .append(
                    new KeyedCodec<>("HomingRange", Codec.DOUBLE, true),
                    (homingProjectileConfig, value) -> homingProjectileConfig.homingRange = value,
                    homingProjectileConfig -> homingProjectileConfig.homingRange
            )
            .documentation("Maximum distance to search for valid targets when locking on.")
            .add()
            .append(
                    new KeyedCodec<>("TurnRateDegreesPerTick", Codec.DOUBLE, true),
                    (homingProjectileConfig, value) -> homingProjectileConfig.turnRateDegreesPerTick = value,
                    homingProjectileConfig -> homingProjectileConfig.turnRateDegreesPerTick
            )
            .documentation("How quickly the projectile can turn toward its target each tick, in degrees.")
            .add()
            .append(
                    new KeyedCodec<>("LockConeAngleDegrees", Codec.DOUBLE, true),
                    (homingProjectileConfig, value) -> homingProjectileConfig.lockConeAngleDegrees = value,
                    homingProjectileConfig -> homingProjectileConfig.lockConeAngleDegrees
            )
            .documentation("Half-angle of the forward lock cone, in degrees, used to decide which targets can be acquired.")
            .add()
            .append(
                    new KeyedCodec<>("ChaseRange", Codec.DOUBLE, true),
                    (homingProjectileConfig, value) -> homingProjectileConfig.chaseRange = value,
                    homingProjectileConfig -> homingProjectileConfig.chaseRange
            )
            .documentation("Distance at which the projectile switches to direct chase and snaps to the target instead of steering smoothly.")
            .add()
            .append(
                    new KeyedCodec<>("ChaseSpeedBonus", Codec.DOUBLE, true),
                    (homingProjectileConfig, value) -> homingProjectileConfig.chaseSpeedBonus = value,
                    homingProjectileConfig -> homingProjectileConfig.chaseSpeedBonus
            )
            .documentation("Extra speed applied while the projectile is in the direct-chase zone.")
            .add()
            .build();

    private boolean isEnabled;
    private double homingRange;
    private double turnRateDegreesPerTick;
    private double lockConeAngleDegrees;
    private double chaseRange;
    private double chaseSpeedBonus;

    // Constructors etc..
}

As you can see its a lot of lines of code. It takes a lot of mental energy to write this and have this chunk of code in your class. And if you want to reference this component in e.g your interaction you'll need to write CODEC again! Thats where the devkit comes in to help you not think about that. Just using few annotations, the code is generated automatically with as less lines of code as possible for you!

docs.title: After Using the library
docs.text:
Example

HomingProjectileComponent.kt
package com.example.exampleplugin.components

import com.hypixel.hytale.codec.builder.BuilderCodec
import com.hypixel.hytale.component.Component
import com.hypixel.hytale.server.core.universe.world.storage.EntityStore
import io.github.dawidprosba.aergiadevkit.ksp.hytalecodec.annotations.CodecProperty
import io.github.dawidprosba.aergiadevkit.ksp.hytalecodec.annotations.GenerateCodec
import io.github.dawidprosba.aergiadevkit.ksp.hytalecodec.api.CodecProvider


@GenerateCodec  // (1)!
class HomingProjectileComponent : Component<EntityStore> {
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "Enable/Disable Homing Feature")  // (2)!
    var isEnabled = false
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "Maximum distance to search for valid targets when locking on.")
    var homingRange = 0.0
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "How quickly the projectile can turn toward its target each tick, in degrees.")
    var turnRateDegreesPerTick = 0.0
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "Half-angle of the forward lock cone, in degrees, used to decide which targets can be acquired.")
    var lockConeAngleDegrees = 0.0
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "Distance at which the projectile switches to direct chase and snaps to the target instead of steering smoothly.")
    var chaseRange = 0.0
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "Extra speed applied while the projectile is in the direct-chase zone.")
    var chaseSpeedBonus = 0.0


    // Constructors etc..


    companion object : CodecProvider<HomingProjectileComponent> {  // (3)!
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<HomingProjectileComponent> = CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent
    }
}
  1. Let generator know that this class needs CODEC generation.
  2. Let generator know that this property is part of the CODEC. The generator will automatically pickup its type! (Yes, there's option to add validators too! I'll talk about it in section dedicated to this.)
  3. Use CodecProvider from io.github.dawidprosba.aergiadevkit.ksp.hytalecodec.api.CodecProvider and register the CODEC by using generated CODEC. The naming convention is CodecBuilder<YourComponentName>, It'll be generated at compile time so you need to run your build. (I'll automate that in the future)

Your codec is generated at Compile Time, meaning you need to compile your code to generate the codec (I want to make this automatic in the future). And then import CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent. You can see how it got generated (But remember to not edit it as it will get overwritten on next compilation):

Generated Codec CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent.kt
// Auto-generated by hytale-aergia-mod-devkit. Changes here will be overwritten, do not edit manually.
package com.example.exampleplugin.components

import com.hypixel.hytale.codec.Codec
import com.hypixel.hytale.codec.KeyedCodec
import com.hypixel.hytale.codec.builder.BuilderCodec

public val CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent: BuilderCodec<HomingProjectileComponent> =
    BuilderCodec.builder(HomingProjectileComponent::class.java, ::HomingProjectileComponent)
    .append(KeyedCodec("IsEnabled", Codec.BOOLEAN, false),
        { config, value -> config.isEnabled = value },
        { config -> config.isEnabled }
    )
    .documentation("Enable/Disable Homing Feature")
    .add()
    .append(KeyedCodec("HomingRange", Codec.DOUBLE, false),
        { config, value -> config.homingRange = value },
        { config -> config.homingRange }
    )
    .documentation("Maximum distance to search for valid targets when locking on.")
    .add()
    .append(KeyedCodec("TurnRateDegreesPerTick", Codec.DOUBLE, false),
        { config, value -> config.turnRateDegreesPerTick = value },
        { config -> config.turnRateDegreesPerTick }
    )
    .documentation("How quickly the projectile can turn toward its target each tick, in degrees.")
    .add()
    .append(KeyedCodec("LockConeAngleDegrees", Codec.DOUBLE, false),
        { config, value -> config.lockConeAngleDegrees = value },
        { config -> config.lockConeAngleDegrees }
    )
    .documentation("Half-angle of the forward lock cone, in degrees, used to decide which targets can be acquired.")
    .add()
    .append(KeyedCodec("ChaseRange", Codec.DOUBLE, false),
        { config, value -> config.chaseRange = value },
        { config -> config.chaseRange }
    )
    .documentation("Distance at which the projectile switches to direct chase and snaps to the target instead of steering smoothly.")
    .add()
    .append(KeyedCodec("ChaseSpeedBonus", Codec.DOUBLE, false),
        { config, value -> config.chaseSpeedBonus = value },
        { config -> config.chaseSpeedBonus }
    )
    .documentation("Extra speed applied while the projectile is in the direct-chase zone.")
    .add()
    .build()

docs.title: Auto Registration
docs.text:
The library also provides automatic registration of Components, Systems and Events (I'm working to extend that to handle all cases).

Lets build on our example from CODEC generation. We want to make so the component is automatically registered when the plugin is loaded without any additional code.

HomingProjectileComponent.kt
// You can pass false as second argument to disable registration of this component!
@RegisterComponent("HomingProjectileComponent") // (1)!
@GenerateCodec
class HomingProjectileComponent : Component<EntityStore> {
    // ... CODE
    companion object : CodecProvider<HomingProjectileComponent>, ComponentTypeProvider<HomingProjectileComponent> { // (2)!
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<HomingProjectileComponent> = CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent
        override var componentType: ComponentType<EntityStore, HomingProjectileComponent>? = null
    }
}
  1. Use @RegisterComponent annotation to register the component. The name you provide in the annotation will be used as the component name in the game. You can reference this component in your interactions or other places using this name.
  2. The companion object is required to provide the CODEC property. Use CodecBuilder to make sure the CODEC value is resolved correctly.

    This part will generate registration code for you, but we need to hook the registry in our plugin setup() method. (This is one time config only as all components will be appended to the generated registry).

    ExamplePlugin.kt
    class ExamplePlugin(init: JavaPluginInit) : JavaPlugin(init) {
    // ... CODE
    override fun setup() {
        ComponentRegistryGenerated.registerAll( // (1)!
            registry = this.entityStoreRegistry
        )
    }
    // ... CODE
}
    1. Just need to add call to the generated registry.

    Generated Registry

    This is how the registry will look like after the registration. If you disable component registration, It'll convert into LOG block and will print information to console. Remember you do not need nor you should edit this file as it will get overwritten on next compilation. All components annotated with @RegisterComponent will be automatically added to this registry! 

    public object ComponentRegistryGenerated {
  public val LOGGER: HytaleLogger = HytaleLogger.get(ExamplePlugin::class.simpleName)

  public fun registerAll(registry: ComponentRegistryProxy<EntityStore>) {
    registerComponent(HomingProjectileComponent::class, registry)
  }
}

docs.title: Annotations
docs.text: Aergia Mod Devkit provides two sets of annotations: codec annotations that generate serialization codecs at compile time, and registry annotations that auto-register mod elements at startup.

docs.title: Codec annotations
docs.text: Annotation Target Description @GenerateCodec class Generates a BuilderCodec for the annotated class @CodecProperty property, value parameter Marks a field for inclusion in the generated codec @CodecRequiredValidator property, value parameter Adds a non-null validator to a codec field @CodecProjectileValidator property, value parameter Adds the Hytale projectile asset validator to a codec field

docs.title: Registry annotations
docs.text: Annotation Target Description @RegisterComponent class Registers a component in the Hytale component registry @RegisterInteraction class Registers an interaction in the codec map registry @RegisterSystem class Registers a system in the entity store registry @RegisterGlobalEvent function Registers a global event listener @RegisterEvent function Registers an entity-scoped event listener

docs.title: @CodecProjectileValidator
docs.text:
Adds the Hytale projectile asset validator to the codec entry for the annotated property. The validator resolves the projectile asset reference against the asset cache using Projectile.VALIDATOR_CACHE.getValidator().late().

Target: property, value parameter Retention: SOURCE (compile-time only)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Place on the same property as @CodecProperty when the field holds a projectile asset identifier that must be validated against the loaded asset cache.

@GenerateCodec
class HomingProjectileComponent : Component<EntityStore> {
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "The projectile asset to home toward.")
    @CodecProjectileValidator
    var projectileId: String = ""

    companion object : CodecProvider<HomingProjectileComponent>, ComponentTypeProvider<HomingProjectileComponent> {
        override var componentType: ComponentType<EntityStore, HomingProjectileComponent>? = null
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<HomingProjectileComponent> =
            CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent
    }
}

docs.title: Generated output
docs.text:
The annotation causes the following to be appended to the codec entry:

.addValidator(Projectile.VALIDATOR_CACHE.getValidator().late())

The .late() call defers validator resolution until the asset cache is populated, which is why this is safe to use with fields referencing runtime-loaded assets.

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• The containing class must be annotated with @GenerateCodec.
  • The property must be annotated with @CodecProperty.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• @CodecProperty — marks the field for codec inclusion
  • @CodecRequiredValidator — alternative validator for non-null checks
  • @GenerateCodec — triggers codec generation for the class

docs.title: @CodecProperty
docs.text:
Marks a var property for inclusion in the codec generated by @GenerateCodec. Each annotated property becomes a keyed entry in the BuilderCodec.

Target: property, value parameter Retention: SOURCE (compile-time only)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description documentation String required Description of the field included in the codec's documentation output required Boolean true Whether the field is required during deserialization

docs.title: Codec key derivation
docs.text:
The codec key is derived from the property name with the first letter uppercased.

isEnabled    → "IsEnabled"
homingRange  → "HomingRange"
projectileId → "ProjectileId"

docs.title: Supported types
docs.text:
Primitive types are mapped directly to Hytale codec primitives:

Kotlin type Codec String Codec.STRING Int Codec.INT Boolean Codec.BOOLEAN Float Codec.FLOAT Double Codec.DOUBLE Long Codec.LONG

For any other type, the processor assumes the type has a CODEC companion property (e.g. HomingProjectileConfig.CODEC).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Fields must be mutable (var). The documentation string is required and must not be blank.

@GenerateCodec
class HomingProjectileComponent : Component<EntityStore> {
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "Enable/Disable Homing Feature")
    var isEnabled = false

    @CodecProperty(documentation = "Maximum distance to search for valid targets when locking on.")
    var homingRange = 0.0

    @CodecProperty(documentation = "The projectile to spawn.", required = false)
    var projectileId: String = ""

    companion object : CodecProvider<HomingProjectileComponent>, ComponentTypeProvider<HomingProjectileComponent> {
        override var componentType: ComponentType<EntityStore, HomingProjectileComponent>? = null
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<HomingProjectileComponent> =
            CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent
    }
}

docs.title: Validators
docs.text:
You can stack a validator annotation on the same property alongside @CodecProperty:

  • @CodecRequiredValidator — asserts the field is non-null
  • @CodecProjectileValidator — validates a projectile asset reference
@CodecProperty(documentation = "The projectile to spawn.")
@CodecRequiredValidator
var projectileId: String = ""

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: The containing class must be annotated with @GenerateCodec.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• @GenerateCodec — triggers codec generation for the class
  • @CodecRequiredValidator — adds a non-null validator
  • @CodecProjectileValidator — adds the projectile asset validator

docs.title: @CodecRequiredValidator
docs.text:
Adds a non-null validator (Validators.nonNull()) to the codec entry for the annotated property. The validator runs during deserialization and rejects null values.

Target: property, value parameter Retention: SOURCE (compile-time only)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Place on the same property as @CodecProperty. The validator is appended after the .documentation(...) call in the generated codec chain.

@GenerateCodec
open class SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction : SimpleInstantInteraction() {
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "The projectile to launch.")
    @CodecRequiredValidator
    var projectileId: String = ""

    companion object : CodecProvider<SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction> {
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction> =
            CodecBuilderSimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction
    }
}

docs.title: Generated output
docs.text:
The annotation causes the following to be appended to the codec entry:

.addValidator(Validators.nonNull())

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• The containing class must be annotated with @GenerateCodec.
  • The property must be annotated with @CodecProperty.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• @CodecProperty — marks the field for codec inclusion
  • @CodecProjectileValidator — alternative validator for projectile asset references
  • @GenerateCodec — triggers codec generation for the class

docs.title: @GenerateCodec
docs.text:
Instructs the KSP processor to generate a BuilderCodec<T> for the annotated class at compile time. The generated codec is used to serialize and deserialize the class from Hytale's codec format.

Target: class Retention: SOURCE (compile-time only)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Generated output
docs.text:
The processor generates a top-level property named CodecBuilder<ClassName> in the same package as the annotated class.

// Generated file: HomingProjectileComponentCodec.kt
val CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent: BuilderCodec<HomingProjectileComponent> = ...

You assign this to CODEC in the companion object — see Required companion object.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
@GenerateCodec
class HomingProjectileComponent : Component<EntityStore> {
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "Enable/Disable Homing Feature")
    var isEnabled = false

    companion object : CodecProvider<HomingProjectileComponent>, ComponentTypeProvider<HomingProjectileComponent> {
        override var componentType: ComponentType<EntityStore, HomingProjectileComponent>? = null
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<HomingProjectileComponent> =
            CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent // (1)!
    }
}
  1. This is generated by the compiler. If it is not resolvable, build or run the project first.

docs.title: Required companion object
docs.text:
The companion object must implement CodecProvider<T> and assign the generated CodecBuilder<ClassName> to CODEC. Without this, the codec cannot be referenced by the registry.

Warning

The processor emits a warning and skips code generation if no @CodecProperty-annotated fields are found on the class.

docs.title: Inheritance
docs.text: If the class inherits from a base class that also has a CODEC in its companion, the generated codec is built with the parent codec and inherited properties use .appendInherited(...) automatically. No extra configuration is needed.

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: @GenerateCodec is required by @RegisterComponent and @RegisterInteraction — both require the class to be serializable.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• @CodecProperty — marks fields for inclusion in the generated codec
  • @CodecRequiredValidator — adds a non-null validator to a field
  • @CodecProjectileValidator — adds the projectile asset validator to a field
  • Component Registry
  • Interaction Registry

docs.title: @RegisterComponent
docs.text:
Registers a Component<EntityStore> subclass in the Hytale component registry. The KSP processor collects all annotated classes and emits ComponentRegistryGenerated, which you call at plugin startup.

Target: class Retention: RUNTIME

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description id String required The string key under which the component is registered enabled Boolean true Set to false to skip registration and log a warning at startup

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
@RegisterComponent("HomingProjectileComponent")
@GenerateCodec
class HomingProjectileComponent : Component<EntityStore> {
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "Enable/Disable Homing Feature")
    var isEnabled = false

    companion object : CodecProvider<HomingProjectileComponent>, ComponentTypeProvider<HomingProjectileComponent> {
        override var componentType: ComponentType<EntityStore, HomingProjectileComponent>? = null
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<HomingProjectileComponent> =
            CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent
    }
}

docs.title: Required companion object
docs.text:
The companion object must implement both CodecProvider<T> and ComponentTypeProvider<T>:

  • CODEC — the generated BuilderCodec<T> (from @GenerateCodec)
  • componentType — initialized to null; auto-populated by the registry during registerAll

docs.title: Disabling
docs.text:
Pass enabled = false to disable registration without removing the class. The runtime logs a warning instead of registering.

@RegisterComponent("HomingProjectileComponent", enabled = false)
@GenerateCodec
class HomingProjectileComponent : Component<EntityStore> { ... }

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• @GenerateCodec — required; all components must be serializable
  • @CodecProperty — required on at least one field for @GenerateCodec to produce output

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• @GenerateCodec — generates the codec for the component
  • @CodecProperty — marks fields for serialization
  • Component Registry — full setup guide and plugin wiring

docs.title: @RegisterEvent
docs.text:
Registers a function as an entity-scoped event listener. The listener fires only when the given event is triggered for the specified subject type (e.g. a particular Item type).

The KSP processor collects all annotated functions and emits EventRegistryGenerated, which you call at plugin startup.

Target: function Retention: RUNTIME

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description eventClass KClass<*> required The event class to listen for subject KClass<*> required The entity or item type to scope the listener to enabled Boolean true Set to false to skip registration and log a warning at startup

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
The function must be inside a companion object. It can optionally accept the event as a parameter.

With event parameterWithout event parameter 
class ExampleEventListener {
    companion object {
        @RegisterEvent(LoadedAssetsEvent::class, Item::class)
        fun onItemsLoaded(event: LoadedAssetsEvent<String, Item, DefaultAssetMap<String, Item>>) {
            val count = event.loadedAssets.count()
            HytaleLogger.getLogger().atInfo().log("$count items loaded!")
        }
    }
}

class ExampleEventListener {
    companion object {
        @RegisterEvent(LoadedAssetsEvent::class, Item::class)
        fun onItemsLoaded() {
            HytaleLogger.getLogger().atInfo().log("Some items were loaded!")
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Disabling
docs.text:
Pass enabled = false to disable registration without removing the function. The runtime logs a warning instead of registering.

@RegisterEvent(LoadedAssetsEvent::class, Item::class, enabled = false)
fun onItemsLoaded() { ... }

docs.title: Difference from @RegisterGlobalEvent
docs.text: @RegisterEvent requires a subject and scopes the listener to that type. Use @RegisterGlobalEvent when the event is not tied to a specific entity or item type.

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: None. No codec annotations are required.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• @RegisterGlobalEvent — global variant with no subject scoping
  • Event Registry — full setup guide and plugin wiring

docs.title: @RegisterGlobalEvent
docs.text:
Registers a function as a global event listener. Global listeners fire for the given event regardless of which entity or subject triggers it.

The KSP processor collects all annotated functions and emits GlobalEventRegistryGenerated, which you call at plugin startup.

Target: function Retention: RUNTIME

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description eventClass KClass<*> required The event class to listen for enabled Boolean true Set to false to skip registration and log a warning at startup

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
The function must be inside a companion object. It can optionally accept the event as a parameter.

With event parameterWithout event parameter 
class ExampleEventListener {
    companion object {
        @RegisterGlobalEvent(PlayerReadyEvent::class)
        fun onPlayerReady(event: PlayerReadyEvent) {
            HytaleLogger.getLogger().atInfo().log("Player is ready: " + event.player.gameMode.name)
        }
    }
}

class ExampleEventListener {
    companion object {
        @RegisterGlobalEvent(PlayerReadyEvent::class)
        fun onPlayerReady() {
            HytaleLogger.getLogger().atInfo().log("Player is ready!")
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Disabling
docs.text:
Pass enabled = false to disable registration without removing the function. The runtime logs a warning instead of registering.

@RegisterGlobalEvent(PlayerReadyEvent::class, false)
fun onPlayerReady(event: PlayerReadyEvent) { ... }

docs.title: Difference from @RegisterEvent
docs.text: @RegisterGlobalEvent does not require a subject — it fires for all instances. Use @RegisterEvent when you need to scope the listener to a specific entity or item type.

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: None. No codec annotations are required.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• @RegisterEvent — entity-scoped variant that also requires a subject parameter
  • Global Event Registry — full setup guide and plugin wiring

docs.title: @RegisterInteraction
docs.text:
Registers an Interaction subclass in the CodecMapRegistry. The KSP processor collects all annotated classes and emits InteractionRegistryGenerated, which you call at plugin startup.

Target: class Retention: RUNTIME

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description id String required The string key under which the interaction is registered enabled Boolean true Set to false to skip registration and log a warning at startup

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
@RegisterInteraction("SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction")
@GenerateCodec
open class SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction : SimpleInstantInteraction() {
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "The projectile to launch.")
    @CodecRequiredValidator
    var projectileId: String = ""

    companion object : CodecProvider<SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction> {
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction> =
            CodecBuilderSimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction // (1)!
    }
}
  1. This is generated by the compiler. The name follows the pattern CodecBuilder<YourInteractionClassName>. If it is not resolvable, build or run the project first.

docs.title: Required companion object
docs.text:
The companion object must implement CodecProvider<T> and assign the generated CodecBuilder<ClassName> to CODEC.

Unlike @RegisterComponent, interactions do not require ComponentTypeProvider<T>.

docs.title: Disabling
docs.text:
Pass enabled = false to disable registration without removing the class. The runtime logs a warning instead of registering.

@RegisterInteraction("SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction", enabled = false)
@GenerateCodec
open class SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction : SimpleInstantInteraction() { ... }

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• @GenerateCodec — required; all interactions must be serializable
  • @CodecProperty — required on at least one field for @GenerateCodec to produce output

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• @GenerateCodec — generates the codec for the interaction
  • @CodecProperty — marks fields for serialization
  • @CodecRequiredValidator — validates required codec fields
  • Interaction Registry — full setup guide and plugin wiring

docs.title: @RegisterSystem
docs.text:
Registers an ISystem<EntityStore> in the entity store registry. Unlike the other registry annotations, no string ID is required — the class itself is the registry key.

The KSP processor collects all annotated classes and emits SystemRegistryGenerated, which you call at plugin startup.

Target: class Retention: RUNTIME

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description enabled Boolean true Set to false to skip registration and log a warning at startup

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
@RegisterSystem()
class HomingMissileTickSystem : EntityTickingSystem<EntityStore>() {
    // ...
}

docs.title: Instantiation
docs.text:
The system is instantiated automatically during registerAll:

  • If the class is a Kotlin object, objectInstance is used.
  • Otherwise, a no-arg constructor is invoked.

No companion object is needed.

docs.title: Disabling
docs.text:
Pass enabled = false to disable registration without removing the class. The runtime logs a warning instead of registering.

@RegisterSystem(enabled = false)
class HomingMissileTickSystem : EntityTickingSystem<EntityStore>() { ... }

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: None. @RegisterSystem does not require @GenerateCodec because systems are not serialized — they are instantiated directly.

docs.title: See also
docs.text: • System Registry — full setup guide and plugin wiring

docs.title: Converting a Hytale Plugin from Java to Kotlin
docs.text:
This guide walks through converting the Hytale Example Plugin boilerplate from Java to Kotlin. If you are starting from that boilerplate, these are the exact steps needed to get a working Kotlin project.

The build system (build.gradle.kts) is already written in Kotlin DSL — only the source files and a few build configuration lines need changing.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• The Hytale Example Plugin boilerplate cloned and building successfully or similarly configured project.
  • IntelliJ IDEA (recommended — it has a built-in Java-to-Kotlin converter)
  • Basic familiarity with Kotlin syntax

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: To convert a Hytale plugin from Java to Kotlin, you need to add Kotlin support into your project and convert your Java files into Kotlin files. (You can do it gradually, one file at a time, and project will still work with mixed Java and Kotlin files.)

docs.title: Adding kotlin support to the project
Step 1: Check what files are in the project
docs.text:
Check if you have build.gradle.kts, settings.gradle.kts or build.gradle and settings.gradle file.

Note

If you have build.gradle.kts and settings.gradle.kts file, you can skip this step.

To convert it to kotlin setting files, you can follow this Migrating from Groovy to Kotlin guide or use AI to handle migration for you. At the end of the guide, is example build.gradle.kts and settings.gradle.kts file if you want to check it out.

docs.title: Step 2: Add Kotlin plugin and Configure kotlin compiler
Step 3: Convert Java Plugin file to Kotlin
docs.text:
Info

The Kotlin files are created under src/main/kotlin/ where java files are src/main/java/. So if your plugin is in src/main/java/com/example/plugin/ExamplePlugin.java, create a new file in src/main/kotlin/com/example/plugin/ExamplePlugin.kt.

Tip

In IntelliJ IDEA, use Code > Convert Java File to Kotlin File to automatically convert your existing Java file, or simply paste Java code into a .kt file and IntelliJ will offer to convert it for you. Afterwards, delete the original .java file to avoid conflicts.

Example Java PluginExample Kotlin Plugin src/main/java/com/example/plugin/ExamplePlugin.java
package com.example.exampleplugin;
import com.hypixel.hytale.logger.HytaleLogger;
import com.hypixel.hytale.server.core.plugin.JavaPlugin;
import com.hypixel.hytale.server.core.plugin.JavaPluginInit;

public class ExamplePlugin extends JavaPlugin {
    private static final HytaleLogger LOGGER = HytaleLogger.forEnclosingClass();

    public ExamplePlugin(JavaPluginInit init) {
        super(init);
        LOGGER.atInfo().log("Hello from %s version %s", this.getName(), this.getManifest().getVersion().toString());
    }

    @Override
    protected void setup() {
        this.getCommandRegistry().registerCommand(new ExampleCommand(this.getName(), this.getManifest().getVersion().toString()));
    }
}
src/main/kotlin/com/example/plugin/ExamplePlugin.kt
package com.example.exampleplugin

import com.hypixel.hytale.logger.HytaleLogger
import com.hypixel.hytale.server.core.plugin.JavaPlugin
import com.hypixel.hytale.server.core.plugin.JavaPluginInit

class ExamplePlugin(init: JavaPluginInit) : JavaPlugin(init) {

    companion object {
        private val LOGGER = HytaleLogger.forEnclosingClass()
    }

    init {
        LOGGER.atInfo().log("Hello from %s version %s using KOTLIN!", name, manifest.version.toString())
    }

    override fun setup() {
        commandRegistry.registerCommand(ExampleCommand(name, manifest.version.toString()))
    }
}

docs.title: Run the Server
docs.text:
You don't need to change rest of the files for now and project should run smoothly. After you run server you should look for Hello from ExamplePlugin version 0.1.0 using KOTLIN! in console.

Example

... OTHER LOGS
[ExamplePlugin] Hello from ExampleGroup:ExamplePlugin version 0.1.0 using KOTLIN!
... OTHER LOGS

docs.title: Example configuration
docs.text:
Info

The configuration is heavily inspired by Example Plugin, the template is already configured with good practices and uses build.gradle.kts and settings.gradle.kts, all you need to do is to add Kotlin support to your project and convert your Java files into Kotlin files.

build.gradle.ktssettings.gradle.kts build.gradle.kts
plugins {
    `maven-publish`
    kotlin("jvm") version "2.3.20"
    id("hytale-mod") version "0.+"
}

group = "com.example"
version = "0.1.0"
val javaVersion = 25

repositories {
    mavenLocal()
    mavenCentral()
    maven("https://central.sonatype.com/repository/maven-snapshots/") {
        name = "SonatypeCentral"
    }
    maven("https://maven.hytale-modding.info/releases") {
        name = "HytaleModdingReleases"
    }
}

dependencies {
    compileOnly(libs.jetbrains.annotations)
    compileOnly(libs.jspecify)
}

hytale {
    // uncomment if you want to add the Assets.zip file to your external libraries;
    // ⚠️ CAUTION, this file is very big and might make your IDE unresponsive for some time!
    //
    // addAssetsDependency = true

    // uncomment if you want to develop your mod against the pre-release version of the game.
    //
    // updateChannel = "pre-release"
}

kotlin {
    jvmToolchain(javaVersion)
}

java {
    withSourcesJar()
}

tasks.named<ProcessResources>("processResources") {
    var replaceProperties = mapOf(
        "plugin_group" to findProperty("plugin_group"),
        "plugin_maven_group" to project.group,
        "plugin_name" to project.name,
        "plugin_version" to project.version,
        "server_version" to findProperty("server_version"),

        "plugin_description" to findProperty("plugin_description"),
        "plugin_website" to findProperty("plugin_website"),

        "plugin_main_entrypoint" to findProperty("plugin_main_entrypoint"),
        "plugin_author" to findProperty("plugin_author")
    )

    filesMatching("manifest.json") {
        expand(replaceProperties)
    }

    inputs.properties(replaceProperties)
}

tasks.withType<Jar> {
    manifest {
        attributes["Specification-Title"] = rootProject.name
        attributes["Specification-Version"] = version
        attributes["Implementation-Title"] = project.name
        attributes["Implementation-Version"] =
            providers.environmentVariable("COMMIT_SHA_SHORT")
                .map { "${version}-${it}" }
                .getOrElse(version.toString())
    }
}

publishing {
    repositories {
        // This is where you put repositories that you want to publish to.
        // Do NOT put repositories for your dependencies here.
    }

    publications {
        create<MavenPublication>("maven") {
            from(components["java"])
        }
    }
}

// IDEA no longer automatically downloads sources/javadoc jars for dependencies, so we need to explicitly enable the behavior.
idea {
    module {
        isDownloadSources = true
        isDownloadJavadoc = true
    }
}

val syncAssets = tasks.register<Copy>("syncAssets") {
    group = "hytale"
    description = "Automatically syncs assets from Build back to Source after server stops."

    // Take from the temporary build folder (Where the game saved changes)
    from(layout.buildDirectory.dir("resources/main"))

    // Copy into your actual project source (Where your code lives)
    into("src/main/resources")

    // IMPORTANT: Protect the manifest template from being overwritten
    exclude("manifest.json")

    // If a file exists, overwrite it with the new version from the game
    duplicatesStrategy = DuplicatesStrategy.INCLUDE

    doLast {
        println("✅ Assets successfully synced from Game to Source Code!")
    }
}

afterEvaluate {
    // Now Gradle will find it, because the plugin has finished working
    val targetTask = tasks.findByName("runServer") ?: tasks.findByName("server")

    if (targetTask != null) {
        targetTask.finalizedBy(syncAssets)
        logger.lifecycle("✅ specific task '${targetTask.name}' hooked for auto-sync.")
    } else {
        logger.warn("⚠️ Could not find 'runServer' or 'server' task to hook auto-sync into.")
    }
}
settings.gradle.kts
pluginManagement {
    repositories {
        gradlePluginPortal()
        mavenCentral()
        maven("https://maven.hytale-modding.info/releases") {
            name = "HytaleModdingReleases"
        }
    }
}

plugins {
    id("org.gradle.toolchains.foojay-resolver-convention") version "1.0.0"
}

rootProject.name = "ExamplePlugin"

Next: Getting Started →

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks through adding and configuring the plugin in your project. If your project is Java project instead of Kotlin, follow this guide Converting Java Plugin to Kotlin to Convert it into Kotlin.

docs.title: Add Plugin to Project Dependencies
Add Configuration For the KSP Processor
docs.text:
Change the registriesOutputPackage and pluginClass to match your project.

build.gradle.kts
// ...
ksp {
    arg("registriesOutputPackage", "com.example.exampleplugin.registries.generated")
    arg("pluginClass", "com.example.exampleplugin.ExamplePlugin")
}

Done!

You can now start using the devkit in your project. On each build It'll generate classes for you.

docs.title: Component Registry
docs.text: Use @RegisterComponent and @GenerateCodec.

docs.title: 1. Annotate your component
docs.text:
Add @RegisterComponent("<your-component-id>") and @GenerateCodec to your component class. The @GenerateCodec annotation is required because all components must be serializable.

Example

@RegisterComponent("HomingProjectileComponent")
@GenerateCodec
class HomingProjectileComponent : Component<EntityStore> {
    //...
}

docs.title: 2. Add a companion object
docs.text:
The companion object must implement both CodecProvider<YourComponent> and ComponentTypeProvider<YourComponent>, which requires you to provide:

  • CODEC — the codec for your component (generated by the compiler)
  • componentType — set to null; auto-populated during registration
@RegisterComponent("HomingProjectileComponent")
@GenerateCodec
class HomingProjectileComponent : Component<EntityStore> {
    companion object : CodecProvider<HomingProjectileComponent>, ComponentTypeProvider<HomingProjectileComponent> {
        override var componentType: ComponentType<EntityStore, HomingProjectileComponent>? = null
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<HomingProjectileComponent> =
            CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent // (1)!
    }
}
  1. This is generated by the compiler. The name follows the pattern CodecBuilder<YourComponentClassName>. If it is not resolvable, build or run the project first.

docs.title: 3. Mark fields for serialization
docs.text:
Annotate each field you want serialized with @CodecProperty. Fields must be mutable (var).

@RegisterComponent("HomingProjectileComponent")
@GenerateCodec
class HomingProjectileComponent : Component<EntityStore> {
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "Enable/Disable Homing Feature")
    var isEnabled = false
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "Maximum distance to search for valid targets when locking on.")
    var homingRange = 0.0
    @CodecProperty(documentation = "How quickly the projectile can turn toward its target each tick, in degrees.")
    var turnRateDegreesPerTick = 0.0
    //...

    companion object : CodecProvider<HomingProjectileComponent>, ComponentTypeProvider<HomingProjectileComponent> {
        override var componentType: ComponentType<EntityStore, HomingProjectileComponent>? = null
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<HomingProjectileComponent> =
            CodecBuilderHomingProjectileComponent
    }
}

docs.title: Register components in your plugin
docs.text:
Once your components are defined, call ComponentRegistryGenerated.registerAll in your plugin's setup method.

Warning

If ComponentRegistryGenerated is not available, either the project has not been built yet or the ksp{} block in build.gradle.kts is misconfigured. Check for compiler errors when running the project.

ExamplePlugin.kt
class ExamplePlugin(init: JavaPluginInit) : JavaPlugin(init) {
    override fun setup() {
        ComponentRegistryGenerated.registerAll(
            registry = this.entityStoreRegistry
        )
    }
}

docs.title: Event Registry
docs.text: Use @RegisterEvent.

docs.title: How to register an event listener
1. Annotate your listener method
docs.text:
Add @RegisterEvent(EventClass::class, TypeParam::class) to a method inside a companion object. The method must be static — the code generator references it directly. It can either receive the event as a parameter or take no parameters at all.

With event parameterWithout event parameter 
class ExampleEventListener {
    companion object {
        @RegisterEvent(LoadedAssetsEvent::class, Item::class)
        fun exampleItemLoadedEventWithParam(event: LoadedAssetsEvent<String, Item, DefaultAssetMap<String, Item>>) {
            val numberOfItems = event.loadedAssets.count()
            HytaleLogger.getLogger().atInfo().log("$numberOfItems items loaded!")
        }
    }
}

class ExampleEventListener {
    companion object {
        @RegisterEvent(LoadedAssetsEvent::class, Item::class)
        fun exampleItemLoadedEventWithoutParam() {
            HytaleLogger.getLogger().atInfo().log("Some items are loaded!")
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Register listeners in your plugin
docs.text:
Call EventRegistryGenerated.registerAll in your plugin's setup method.

Warning

If EventRegistryGenerated is not available, either the project has not been built yet or the ksp{} block in build.gradle.kts is misconfigured. Check for compiler errors when running the project.

ExamplePlugin.kt
class ExamplePlugin(init: JavaPluginInit) : JavaPlugin(init) {
    override fun setup() {
        EventRegistryGenerated.registerAll(
            registry = this.eventRegistry
        )
    }
}

docs.title: Global Event Registry
docs.text: Use @RegisterGlobalEvent.

docs.title: How to register a global event listener
1. Annotate your listener method
docs.text:
Add @RegisterGlobalEvent(EventClass::class) to a method inside a companion object. The method must be static — the code generator references it directly. It can either receive the event as a parameter or take no parameters at all.

With event parameterWithout event parameter 
class ExampleEventListener {
    companion object {
        @RegisterGlobalEvent(PlayerReadyEvent::class)
        fun examplePlayerReadyEventWithParam(event: PlayerReadyEvent) {
            HytaleLogger.getLogger().atInfo().log("Player is ready with gamemode -> " + event.player.gameMode.name)
        }
    }
}

class ExampleEventListener {
    companion object {
        @RegisterGlobalEvent(PlayerReadyEvent::class)
        fun examplePlayerReadyEventWithoutParam() {
            HytaleLogger.getLogger().atInfo().log("Player is ready!")
        }
    }
}

docs.title: 2. Disabling a listener
docs.text:
Pass false as the second argument to disable a listener without removing it. The code generator will skip registration and log a warning at startup.

@RegisterGlobalEvent(PlayerReadyEvent::class, false)
fun examplePlayerReadyEventWithParam(event: PlayerReadyEvent) {
    HytaleLogger.getLogger().atInfo().log("Player is ready with gamemode -> " + event.player.gameMode.name)
}

docs.title: Register listeners in your plugin
docs.text:
Call GlobalEventRegistryGenerated.registerAll in your plugin's setup method.

Warning

If GlobalEventRegistryGenerated is not available, either the project has not been built yet or the ksp{} block in build.gradle.kts is misconfigured. Check for compiler errors when running the project.

ExamplePlugin.kt
class ExamplePlugin(init: JavaPluginInit) : JavaPlugin(init) {
    override fun setup() {
        GlobalEventRegistryGenerated.registerAll(
            registry = this.eventRegistry
        )
    }
}

docs.title: Interaction Registry
docs.text: Use @RegisterInteraction and @GenerateCodec.

docs.title: 1. Annotate your interaction class
docs.text:
Add @RegisterInteraction("<your-interaction-id>") and @GenerateCodec to your interaction class.

@RegisterInteraction("SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction")
@GenerateCodec
open class SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction : SimpleInstantInteraction() {
    //...
}

docs.title: 2. Add a companion object
docs.text:
The companion object must implement CodecProvider<YourInteraction> and provide the generated CODEC.

@RegisterInteraction("SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction")
@GenerateCodec
open class SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction : SimpleInstantInteraction() {
    companion object : CodecProvider<SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction> {
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction> =
            CodecBuilderSimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction // (1)!
    }
}
  1. This is generated by the compiler. The name follows the pattern CodecBuilder<YourInteractionClassName>. If it is not resolvable, build or run the project first.

docs.title: 3. Mark fields for serialization
docs.text:
Annotate each field you want serialized with @CodecProperty. Fields must be mutable (var).

@RegisterInteraction("SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction")
@GenerateCodec
open class SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction : SimpleInstantInteraction() {
    companion object : CodecProvider<SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction> {
        override val CODEC: BuilderCodec<SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction> =
            CodecBuilderSimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction
    }

    @CodecProperty(documentation = "The projectile to launch.")
    @CodecRequiredValidator
    var projectileId: String = ""
}

docs.title: 4. Disabling an interaction
docs.text:
Pass enabled = false to disable an interaction without removing it. The code generator will skip registration and log a warning at startup.

@RegisterInteraction("SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction", enabled = false)
@GenerateCodec
open class SimpleProjectileLaunchInteraction : SimpleInstantInteraction() {
    //...
}

docs.title: Register interactions in your plugin
docs.text:
Call InteractionRegistryGenerated.registerAll in your plugin's setup method.

Warning

If InteractionRegistryGenerated is not available, either the project has not been built yet or the ksp{} block in build.gradle.kts is misconfigured. Check for compiler errors when running the project.

ExamplePlugin.kt
class ExamplePlugin(init: JavaPluginInit) : JavaPlugin(init) {
    override fun setup() {
        InteractionRegistryGenerated.registerAll(
            registry = this.getCodecRegistry(Interaction.CODEC)
        )
    }
}

docs.title: System Registry
docs.text: Use @RegisterSystem.

docs.title: 1. Annotate your system class
docs.text:
Add @RegisterSystem() to your system class. Unlike other registries, no ID is required — the class itself is registered directly.

@RegisterSystem()
class HomingMissileTickSystem : EntityTickingSystem<EntityStore>() {
    //...
}

docs.title: 2. Disabling a system
docs.text:
Pass enabled = false to disable a system without removing it. The code generator will skip registration and log a warning at startup.

@RegisterSystem(enabled = false)
class HomingMissileTickSystem : EntityTickingSystem<EntityStore>() {
    //...
}

docs.title: Register systems in your plugin
docs.text:
Call SystemRegistryGenerated.registerAll in your plugin's setup method.

Warning

If SystemRegistryGenerated is not available, either the project has not been built yet or the ksp{} block in build.gradle.kts is misconfigured. Check for compiler errors when running the project.

ExamplePlugin.kt
class ExamplePlugin(init: JavaPluginInit) : JavaPlugin(init) {
    override fun setup() {
        SystemRegistryGenerated.registerAll(
            registry = this.entityStoreRegistry
        )
    }
}
